market_summary: US equity futures are pointing to a lower open, extending yesterday's sell-off, as a potent mix of rising oil prices, surging bond yields, and Big Tech capex concerns dominate the pre-market narrative. The 10-year Treasury yield hit its highest level since January 2025, while Brent crude closed above $100 for the first time since May, reigniting inflation fears and challenging the 'higher-for-longer' rate narrative. Overnight, earnings from Tesla and Alphabet disappointed on margins and capex, respectively, adding to the tech sector's woes. The market is bracing for a volatile open with key economic data and more earnings on the docket.
macro_summary: Stagflation Scare & Regime Change The market is pricing in a higher-for-longer rate environment due to an oil-driven inflation shock, challenging equity valuations and favoring energy and defense over growth and tech. The market is reacting to a sudden and sharp repricing of inflation and rate expectations, triggered by oil's surge past $100. This has broken the recent calm and is forcing a reassessment of the 'soft landing' narrative, pushing the market into a risk-off, stagflationary scare regime. The macro outlook has darkened considerably overnight. The combination of oil above $100 and a surging 10-year yield presents a stagflationary scare for markets. This challenges the 'soft landing' narrative and forces a repricing of Fed rate expectations. The market is now pricing in a higher probability of a rate hike, a stark contrast to the cuts expected earlier this year. The focus is on whether this is a temporary spike or the start of a sustained inflationary trend.
risk_summary: Risk appetite is extremely low. The VIX is likely to open elevated, reflecting high anxiety. Market breadth is expected to be negative, with decliners outpacing advancers. The primary risk is a further unwind of the AI trade, which has been a key market leader. A secondary risk is a disorderly move in the bond market, which could trigger a broader sell-off. Sentiment is cautious, with investors seeking clarity on the oil price trajectory and its impact on corporate earnings.

top_events.title: Brent Crude Oil Closes Above $100 for First Time Since May
top_events.summary: Brent crude oil futures settled above $100 a barrel, driven by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and concerns over supply disruptions. This marks a significant milestone and reignites global inflation fears.

top_events.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Hits Highest Since January 2025
top_events.summary: The yield on the benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury note surged to its highest level in over 18 months, driven by the oil price spike and its implications for inflation. This move tightens financial conditions significantly.

top_events.title: Trump Says US Will Hold Iran Responsible for Houthi Attacks
top_events.summary: President Trump stated that the U.S. will hold Iran responsible for Houthi attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea, following a reported attack on two Saudi tankers. This significantly escalates the risk of a broader regional conflict.

top_events.title: Global oil prices cross $100 a barrel as Iran war sends fuel prices surging
top_events.summary: Global oil prices have surged past $100 per barrel for the first time since May, driven by escalating conflict involving Iran.

top_events.title: Tesla Misses Earnings, Free Cash Flow Turns Negative
top_events.summary: Tesla reported Q2 earnings that missed analyst expectations, with automotive margins declining and free cash flow turning negative. CEO Elon Musk preached patience on robotaxi and Optimus rollouts, disappointing investors.

top_events.title: Alphabet Shares Fall on Massive Capex, Negative Free Cash Flow
top_events.summary: Alphabet's stock fell sharply after the company reported a massive increase in capital spending, leading to negative free cash flow. A JPMorgan note suggested Google's 2027 capex could approach $400 billion, stoking fears of over-investment in AI.

top_events.title: Global Oil Price Hits $100 a Barrel for First Time Since May
top_events.summary: The global benchmark oil price has reached $100 per barrel for the first time since May, driven by supply concerns.

top_events.title: Lockheed Martin and RTX Jump on Earnings Beat
top_events.summary: Lockheed Martin and RTX both reported earnings that beat analyst expectations, driven by strong demand amid elevated geopolitical tensions. Lockheed Martin's stock is breaking out to new highs.

sector_summary: Positive sectors/themes: Energy (XLE), Defense (ITA), Select Healthcare (XLV); Negative sectors/themes: Technology (XLK), Consumer Discretionary (XLY), Airlines (JETS), Real Estate (XLRE); Themes to watch: AI Capex vs. Returns: The market is now scrutinizing the ROI of massive AI spending, which could lead to a prolonged period of underperformance for mega-cap tech., Stagflation Scare: The combination of rising inflation (oil) and slowing growth (housing, consumer) is the most feared macro environment for equities., Defensive Rotation: Capital is rotating from growth to value and from cyclical to defensive sectors like energy, healthcare, and staples.
